dependencies.description: IGMP signals group membership from hosts to the first-hop router. Without IGMP joins, PIM has no downstream interest.
dependencies.name: IGMP

dependencies.description: PIM uses the unicast routing table for RPF checks. Multicast forwarding depends on unicast reachability to sources and RP.
dependencies.name: Unicast Routing (OSPF/BGP/IS-IS/EIGRP)

dependencies.description: PIM must be explicitly enabled. On NX-OS, 'feature pim' enables the process; interfaces need 'ip pim sparse-mode'. On IOS-XE, 'ip multicast-routing' globally plus 'ip pim sparse-mode' per interface.
dependencies.name: feature pim (NX-OS) / ip pim sparse-mode (IOS-XE)

dependencies.description: PIM-SM requires an RP for shared tree operation. Without RP, only SSM (Source-Specific Multicast) groups in 232.0.0.0/8 will work.
dependencies.name: Rendezvous Point (RP)

failure_modes.description: Source is sending multicast traffic and receivers have joined (IGMP groups present) but data is not flowing end-to-end.

resolutions.action: Check RPF with 'show ip pim route' — verify RPF interface points toward source

Check PIM neighbors along the path with 'show ip pim neighbor'
Check PIM neighbors along the path with 'show ip pim neighbor'

Verify MFIB programming with 'show forwarding multicast route'

Check CoPP with 'show policy-map interface control-plane' for PIM/multicast drops

root_causes.cause: RPF failure — traffic arriving on wrong interface, dropped by RPF check
RP unreachable — shared tree cannot be built, no (*,G) join reaches RP
PIM not enabled on an interface in the path — breaks the multicast tree
Missing PIM neighbor on a transit link — joins cannot propagate
MFIB (hardware) not programmed — software has route but ASIC doesn't
ACL or CoPP dropping PIM (protocol 103) or multicast data packets

failure_modes.scenario: No multicast traffic reaching receivers despite active source
symptoms: show ip mroute shows (*,G) or (S,G) but packet counters are zero
show ip igmp groups shows the group on receiver interface
Receivers report no multicast stream
show ip mroute summary shows zero packets

failure_modes.description: Multicast packets are arriving on an interface that is not the RPF interface toward the source. Packets are dropped by the RPF check, causing traffic loss for downstream receivers.

resolutions.action: Compare 'show ip pim route' RPF with unicast route to source (show ip route <source>)
Compare 'show ip pim route' RPF with unicast route to source (show ip route <source>)
Compare 'show ip pim route' RPF with unicast route to source (show ip route <source>)

root_causes.cause: Asymmetric routing — unicast and multicast paths diverge
Static mroute ('ip mroute') overriding unicast RPF with wrong interface
Routing table change — IGP reconvergence caused RPF interface to change, stale MFIB entry
PIM not enabled on the new RPF interface after topology change
ECMP — multicast RPF picks one path, but traffic arrives on the other

Traffic loss starts after routing change or topology event

failure_modes.description: PIM Hello messages not being exchanged between two routers on a shared link. Without PIM adjacency, join/prune messages cannot be sent, breaking the multicast tree across this link.

resolutions.action: Verify 'feature pim' enabled and 'ip pim sparse-mode' on interface (both sides)
Verify 'feature pim' enabled and 'ip pim sparse-mode' on interface (both sides)
Verify 'feature pim' enabled and 'ip pim sparse-mode' on interface (both sides)

Run 'show ip pim config-sanity' for configuration validation
Run 'show ip pim config-sanity' for configuration validation

root_causes.cause: PIM not enabled on one or both sides of the link
ACL or CoPP dropping PIM Hello (IP protocol 103, destination 224.0.0.13)
Interface down or in error state
VRF mismatch — PIM configured in different VRFs on each side
Layer 2 issue — 224.0.0.13 being filtered by IGMP snooping misconfiguration

show ip pim interface shows zero neighbors on the affected interface

show ip pim statistics shows zero hello RX on the interface

For static RP: verify consistent 'ip pim rp-address' config on all routers

root_causes.cause: RP not configured or static RP pointing to wrong address
BSR/Auto-RP not propagating RP information to all routers
RP address not reachable via unicast routing
RP-to-group mapping mismatch — different static configs on different routers
BSR border configured on an interface blocking RP advertisements
RP overloaded or PIM process down on RP router

show ip pim rp shows no RP for the group range
show ip pim rp-hash <group> returns different RPs on different routers
Source can send but receivers never get traffic (no shared tree to RP)

Programming failure between MRIB and MFIB (software bug)
Stale MFIB entry after rapid route changes
Module communication failure on modular chassis — route not pushed to linecard

failure_modes.description: Hosts are sending IGMP joins but the first-hop L3 router is not creating IGMP group state. Typically an L2/snooping issue between host and router.

resolutions.action: Verify PIM enabled on SVI: 'show ip pim interface' should list the SVI
Verify PIM enabled on SVI: 'show ip pim interface' should list the SVI
Verify PIM enabled on SVI: 'show ip pim interface' should list the SVI
Check snooping mrouter ports: 'show ip igmp snooping mrouter' — router port must be listed
Check snooping mrouter ports: 'show ip igmp snooping mrouter' — router port must be listed
Check snooping mrouter ports: 'show ip igmp snooping mrouter' — router port must be listed
Verify IGMP version: 'show ip igmp interface <intf>' — match host IGMP version
Verify IGMP version: 'show ip igmp interface <intf>' — match host IGMP version
Verify IGMP version: 'show ip igmp interface <intf>' — match host IGMP version
Check join policies: 'show ip igmp policy statistics reports' for deny counters
Check join policies: 'show ip igmp policy statistics reports' for deny counters
Check join policies: 'show ip igmp policy statistics reports' for deny counters
Confirm snooping is passing joins: 'show ip igmp snooping statistics' for join RX counters
Confirm snooping is passing joins: 'show ip igmp snooping statistics' for join RX counters
Confirm snooping is passing joins: 'show ip igmp snooping statistics' for join RX counters

root_causes.cause: IGMP snooping filtering/dropping IGMP joins before they reach the router
No mrouter port configured or detected — switch not forwarding joins to router
IGMP version mismatch — host sending IGMPv3 but router expects IGMPv2 (or vice versa)
IGMP join policy (access-group/route-map) filtering the group on the router interface
PIM not enabled on the SVI/interface — IGMP won't be active
VLAN/SVI mismatch — host in wrong VLAN or SVI not created

failure_modes.scenario: IGMP joins not reaching the L3 PIM router
symptoms: show ip igmp groups on the router shows no entry for the group
Host application shows it has joined the group
show ip igmp snooping groups may or may not show the entry
show ip igmp snooping mrouter shows no mrouter port

messages.description: Periodic neighbor discovery and capability exchange on PIM-enabled interfaces
messages.name: Hello
messages.when_sent: Every Hello Interval (default 30s) on each PIM-enabled interface

messages.description: Request to join or prune a multicast group. Sent toward RP for (*,G) or toward source for (S,G).
messages.name: Join/Prune
messages.when_sent: When IGMP join/leave triggers PIM state change, or periodically to refresh (every 60s)

messages.description: Unicast encapsulation of multicast data from FHR to RP. Used to notify RP of an active source.
messages.name: Register
messages.when_sent: When FHR detects multicast source on directly-connected network and has no (S,G) SPT state

messages.description: Sent by RP to FHR to stop Register encapsulation. RP has either joined SPT or has no receivers.
messages.name: Register-Stop
messages.when_sent: When RP receives Register and either has no receivers or has already joined the SPT to source

messages.description: Sent when multiple routers on a multi-access network are forwarding the same multicast stream. Elects a single forwarder based on metric.
messages.name: Assert
messages.when_sent: When a router receives a multicast packet on an outgoing interface (indicates duplicate forwarding)

messages.description: Bootstrap Router messages for dynamic RP-to-group mapping distribution
key_fields: RP-Set (RP address, group range, RP priority)
messages.name: Bootstrap (BSR)
messages.when_sent: Periodically by the elected BSR to distribute RP-set to all PIM routers (destination 224.0.0.13)

messages.description: Sent by Candidate-RPs to the BSR to announce willingness to serve as RP for group ranges
messages.name: Candidate-RP-Advertisement
messages.when_sent: Periodically by configured Candidate-RPs to the BSR unicast address

purpose: Protocol Independent Multicast - Sparse Mode. Builds shared trees rooted at a Rendezvous Point (RP) and source trees (SPT) for multicast forwarding. Used when receivers are sparsely distributed.

states.description: No state for this (S,G) or (*,G) — no downstream receivers and no local interest. Router has no PIM join state and is not part of the multicast tree for this group.
states.name: NO_INFO

states.description: Router has joined the multicast tree. A (*,G) or (S,G) join has been sent upstream toward the RP (shared tree) or source (shortest-path tree). Router is actively receiving or expecting to receive traffic for this group.
states.name: JOIN

states.description: A prune has been received from a downstream neighbor. Waiting for Prune-Pending timer (typically 3 seconds) before removing state — allows other downstream routers on the same LAN to override with a join.
states.name: PRUNE_PENDING

states.description: Source's first-hop router (FHR) is encapsulating multicast data in PIM Register messages and unicasting them to the RP. Waiting for Register-Stop from RP to stop encapsulation.
states.name: REGISTER_SENT

states.description: FHR received Register-Stop from RP. Register encapsulation has stopped. A Register-Probe is sent periodically (every Register-Suppress-Time minus Probe-Time) to check if RP still doesn't need encapsulated data.
states.name: REGISTER_SUPPRESSED

states.description: Router is switching from shared tree (*,G via RP) to shortest-path tree (S,G direct to source). An (S,G) join is sent toward the source while (*,G) state is maintained until SPT traffic arrives.
states.name: SPT_SWITCHOVER

timers.description: Interval between PIM Hello messages on each PIM-enabled interface
timers.name: Hello Interval

os_defaults.notes: Configure with 'ip pim hello-interval' under interface config
Configure with 'ip pim query-interval' under interface config

timers.tuning_advice: Lower values speed up neighbor detection but add overhead. 30s is appropriate for most networks.

timers.description: Time to wait before declaring a PIM neighbor dead after last Hello received
timers.name: Neighbor Holdtime
timers.on_expiry: PIM neighbor declared dead, all join state via that neighbor is removed, tree rebuilt
timers.tuning_advice: Default is 3.5x hello interval. Reducing below 3x risks false neighbor-down in lossy environments.

timers.description: Interval for periodic Join/Prune message refresh to upstream neighbor
timers.name: Join/Prune Interval
timers.on_expiry: Send Join/Prune refresh upstream to maintain multicast tree state

os_defaults.notes: Configure with 'ip pim jp-interval' in PIM config

timers.tuning_advice: Upstream neighbor expects refresh within join holdtime (default 210s = 3.5 x 60s). Do not reduce below 10s.

timers.description: How long upstream router holds join state before timing out if no refresh received
timers.name: Join/Prune Holdtime
timers.on_expiry: Join state expires, router prunes itself from the tree. Traffic loss for downstream receivers.
timers.tuning_advice: Automatically set to 3.5x Join/Prune Interval. Do not set manually unless debugging.

timers.description: Time FHR suppresses Register encapsulation after receiving Register-Stop from RP
timers.name: Register-Suppress Timer
timers.on_expiry: FHR sends Register-Probe (null Register) to check if RP still wants suppression
timers.tuning_advice: Do not change. Mismatched values between FHR and RP cause periodic traffic loss.

timers.description: Delay between receiving a Prune and actually removing the interface from OIF list (allows join override on multi-access networks)
timers.name: Prune-Pending Timer
timers.on_expiry: Interface removed from OIF list, traffic stops flowing to that downstream neighbor
timers.tuning_advice: Should be less than Join/Prune Interval. Do not change unless debugging.

timers.description: Data rate threshold that triggers switchover from shared tree to shortest-path tree. When exceeded, router sends (S,G) join toward source.
timers.name: SPT-Threshold
timers.on_expiry: Not timer-based — triggered when data rate exceeds threshold

os_defaults.notes: Configure with 'ip pim spt-threshold infinity group-list <prefix-list>' to prevent SPT switchover for specific groups

timers.tuning_advice: Default 0 (immediate) is recommended. 'infinity' keeps traffic on shared tree permanently.

transitions.trigger: IGMP join received from host, or PIM join received from downstream router
PIM prune received from downstream neighbor on a multi-access network
Prune-Pending timer expires with no join override received
Join override received from another downstream neighbor before Prune-Pending timer expires
Last downstream receiver leaves (IGMP leave or PIM prune) and join timer expires

transitions.failure_if_stuck: Source detected but Register not sent — check if RP is reachable and PIM enabled on source interface
transitions.trigger: First-hop router detects multicast source sending on a directly connected network

transitions.failure_if_stuck: Register-Stop never arrives — check RP reachability, ACLs blocking unicast Register packets to RP, or RP not running PIM
transitions.trigger: Register-Stop received from RP (RP has built SPT or has no receivers)
Register-Suppress timer expires, Register-Probe sent, no Register-Stop received

transitions.failure_if_stuck: SPT switchover not completing — check RPF to source, PIM adjacency toward source
transitions.trigger: First (S,G) data packet received on shared tree and SPT-threshold exceeded (NX-OS default: immediate switchover, threshold 0)
SPT join reaches source's FHR, (S,G) traffic arrives on SPT

transport: IP protocol 103 (destination 224.0.0.13 for hellos, unicast for register)

config_examples:
ip multicast-routing
interface Vlan100
  ip pim sparse-mode
ip pim rp-address 10.1.1.1

notes.note: Requires 'ip multicast-routing' global config and 'ip pim sparse-mode' on each interface. IOS-XE has 'show ip rpf <source>' which NX-OS does not. 'show ip mroute count' for statistics instead of NX-OS 'show ip mroute summary'.

config_examples:
feature pim
interface Vlan100
  ip pim sparse-mode
ip pim rp-address 10.1.1.1

notes.note: Requires 'feature pim' to enable. PIM automatically enables IGMP on PIM-enabled interfaces. SPT-threshold default is 0 (immediate switchover). There is NO 'show ip rpf' command — use 'show ip pim route' or 'show routing multicast' for RPF verification.
For multicast in VXLAN/EVPN fabrics with TRM (Tenant Routed Multicast), PIM operates in the underlay AND overlay. Check 'show forwarding distribution multicast vxlan mdt-db' for MDT tunnel state.
On modular chassis (N9K-C9500 series), verify multicast route programming per linecard module: 'show forwarding multicast route module <mod>'. Programming failures may be isolated to one module.
